defects.summary: Money aggregated as IEEE-754 float across Fund; totals can drift by a cent
defects.reproduction: Sum [0.1, 0.2] via reduce(+) -> 0.30000000000000004; roll up N balances
defects.root_cause: No deterministic money primitive; ad-hoc float arithmetic
defects.fix: Added src/lib/fund/money.ts (integer minor units + exact sumMoney/sumBy); routed safeMoney through it
defects.verification: All listed aggregation sites now route through sumBy/sumMoney; net-worth combinations use sumMoney; 427 tests green, tsc clean
defects.similar_code_search: All reduce(+ Number()) money aggregation sites in Fund adopted in wave 4.2 (OverviewModule, FundNetWorthModule, FundLiabilities, FundRecurringList, FundCashflowModule, financeDailyJobs, usePlaidConnection)

defects.summary: safeMoney accepted sub-cent floats without rounding to the currency minor unit
Make webhook delivery treated every HTTP response as success
defects.reproduction: Return HTTP 503 from an env-configured Make webhook; the previous triggerWebhook resolved and notifyViaMake wrote result=success
defects.root_cause: The outbound helper parsed response text without checking Response.ok and exposed no typed delivery contract
defects.fix: Return the shared Result<MakeWebhookReceipt>, accept only 2xx, disable redirects, and write normalized failure audit metadata
defects.verification: HTTP 503 maps to provider_error, no success audit is written, focused tests and full 737-test suite pass

defects.summary: Raw Make response/error content could enter exceptions and persistent audit metadata
defects.reproduction: Return a provider body containing a token-like value or throw an error containing the opaque hook path; previous code embedded raw text in MakeError/audit_logs
defects.root_cause: Provider response bodies and thrown messages were treated as safe observability content
defects.fix: Never read webhook response bodies; emit generic structured errors and persist only code/status/retryability/idempotency metadata
defects.verification: Secret-bearing response/error fixtures are absent from Results, Sentry messages, and audit rows; independent review found no substantiated regression

defects.summary: Failed Make writes had no durable replay state and duplicate prevention depended entirely on each remote scenario
defects.reproduction: Return a provider failure or lose the response after Make accepts a write; the prior implementation retained only audit metadata and could not safely claim or replay the payload
defects.root_cause: No durable encrypted write outbox, local dedupe record, or compare-and-swap replay claim
defects.fix: Persist purpose-separated AES-GCM payload envelopes behind metadata-only owner RLS, hash dedupe keys, claim attempts atomically, dead-letter bounded failures, and allow only explicit authenticated operator replay
defects.verification: 17 focused tests pass; local and hosted migrations verify RLS/grants; duplicate, tamper, failed-delivery, third-attempt dead-letter, and replay-success paths are covered

defects.source: Hosted Supabase security advisor after wave 10.14 DDL
defects.summary: Anonymous and authenticated Data API roles could execute the SECURITY DEFINER expire_stale_approvals RPC across all users
defects.reproduction: has_function_privilege returned true and the advisor reported public/authenticated SECURITY DEFINER execution
defects.root_cause: The cron used a browser-facing Supabase client and the RPC retained default PUBLIC plus authenticated EXECUTE grants
defects.fix: Run maintenance RPCs with the service-role client and revoke EXECUTE from PUBLIC, anon, and authenticated while granting service_role only
defects.verification: Local and hosted has_function_privilege: anon=false, authenticated=false, service_role=true; security advisor warning cleared

defects.source: Phase 10 Make management API review plus current official scenario API reference
defects.summary: Make management helpers threw unstructured errors, trusted unvalidated provider data, retried no reads, accepted invalid configuration, and sent scenario inputs under the wrong field
defects.reproduction: Call runScenario with input and inspect the old request body: it used `input`, while Make API v2 requires `data`; malformed team ids became NaN and every management failure escaped as MakeError
defects.root_cause: The older management client predated the shared Result contract, runtime response schemas, provider timing policy, and the current Make scenario-input API
defects.fix: Return schema-validated Result values for list/detail/run/start/stop; validate configuration and ids; reject redirects; retry reads at most three times; attempt writes once; use `{responsive:true,data}`; require id/state activation confirmation; emit only normalized safe failures
defects.verification: 49 focused tests pass; 757-test full suite passes; configured Make list/detail reads passed live id/team/state shape checks without exposing scenario content; no live write performed

defects.source: Platform audit L2/I4 and Phase 10 Composio cache synchronization review
defects.summary: Mail first paint blocked on live provider fan-out and a failed account could not retain durable last-known inbox state
defects.reproduction: Open /mail with multiple direct or Composio accounts while one provider is slow or unavailable; the prior GET /api/mail/inbox waited on every provider and had no persistent rows to render offline
defects.root_cause: Inbox list metadata had no owner-scoped cache or per-account sync state; provider refresh and first paint were the same request
defects.fix: Serve normalized header/snippet metadata from owner-RLS cache, revalidate explicitly through POST /api/mail/sync, retain failed-account rows during partial refresh, record normalized sync outcomes, and keep message bodies live-only
defects.verification: Focused Mail/cache/sync/disconnect tests pass; 768-test full suite and seeded authenticated Mail smoke pass; local and hosted migration/RLS/owner-isolation checks pass

defects.summary: Concentration routine requests accepted malformed JSON shapes and silently replaced explicit unsafe thresholds with a default
defects.reproduction: POST JSON null or an explicit maxWeight below zero or above one to /api/routines/concentration-check; the previous unvalidated body access could destabilize error handling or continue with 25 percent without telling the caller
defects.root_cause: The route cast arbitrary JSON to a request type and treated validation failure as absence instead of distinguishing omitted from invalid input
defects.fix: Parse a strict Zod request contract, require finite maxWeight in (0, 1], require UUID run ids, and return visible INVALID_ROUTINE_INPUT 400 responses before any database or routine work
defects.verification: Malformed null and unsafe threshold cases return 400 with no profile query or routine execution; valid request/profile/default provenance cases remain covered

defects.summary: The deterministic signal severity model was not consumed by Dispatch and did not recognize the application's persisted hi priority vocabulary
defects.reproduction: Persist an action signal with metadata.ai_priority=hi; the prior UI grouped it as Action and deriveSeverity returned actionable instead of critical. Archive a title and resurface a punctuation variant; the queue did not use that resolution history to demote it.
defects.root_cause: Phase 7 shipped pure severity and normalized dedup primitives without wiring them into the existing Dispatch filters/groups, and its urgent label set used high but not the repository's hi enum
defects.fix: Recognize hi as urgent, derive every queue row's tier from persisted type/priority, group and filter by the four Need-to-Know tiers, and feed resolved routed/dismissed/archived titles into normalized duplicate detection while excluding the current record
defects.verification: Focused tests cover hi escalation and punctuation-varied resolution memory; synthetic local desktop/mobile verification showed one row in each tier and responsive filter wrapping

defects.summary: A transient remote preference read failure enables a later local upsert that can overwrite valid server preferences; the Phase 9 envelope also omits current-main timezone state
defects.reproduction: Make the initial user_preferences read fail while local interface settings exist; remoteReady becomes true and the save effect writes the local envelope. On the Phase 9 branch that envelope is only {theme,settings}, so merging without preservation can also erase timeZone used for server local-day calculations.
defects.root_cause: Preference sync treats read completion as read success and replaces the outer JSON envelope instead of versioning/merging known fields while preserving unknown ones
defects.fix: Planned Wave 15.1: merge origin/main, gate automatic writes on successful remote read, preserve unknown envelope fields including timeZone, add versioned legacy Envoy migration, expose retry, and add regression tests

defects.summary: Approval-gated routine resume consumes approval before resumed work succeeds, so a transient failure can strand a resumable run with already-executed authority
defects.reproduction: Resume an approval-gated routine and force a transient executor failure after approval consumption. The route returns a resumable failure but the approval remains executed.
defects.root_cause: Approval consumption precedes retry-safe execution-state persistence and failure handling
defects.fix: Canonical repair exists as direct Phase 9 child c2de9308; fast-forward it in Wave 15.1, then retain its retry contract while integrating main
defects.regression_test: src/lib/routines/resumeExecutor.test.ts plus route retry tests from c2de9308
defects.verification: pending branch integration and independent rerun

defects.summary: Agent-task and approval transitions use stale read-then-write updates without expected-state compare-and-set, allowing concurrent valid requests to overwrite one another
defects.reproduction: Issue two legal transitions from the same task status, or concurrent approve/deny decisions for one pending approval. Both requests can pass the stale precondition and the later update wins without a 409.
defects.root_cause: Transition validation and persistence are separate operations; updates do not constrain the expected prior status or run in an atomic RPC
defects.fix: Planned Wave 15.1: expected-state compare-and-set or fixed-search-path RPC, 409 on conflict, and atomic audit/activity persistence where feasible
defects.regression_test: pending Wave 15.1 concurrent transition route/RPC tests

defects.summary: Task activity, routine steps, and linked approval query failures are discarded, so the UI can present plausible empty or idle data instead of a degraded/error state
defects.reproduction: Force the task-activity, routine-step, or linked-approval query to fail while its parent task/run loads. Current handlers/components continue with empty arrays or omit the approval.
defects.root_cause: Best-effort secondary reads have no typed degradation contract and client catches discard safe failure metadata
defects.fix: Planned Wave 15.4: one owner-scoped active-work projection with explicit section degradation, bounded refresh, deterministic ranking, exact deep links, visible Sync error, and safe Sentry metadata
defects.regression_test: pending Wave 15.4 active-work adapter and browser degradation tests

defects.summary: Intel passes an unrestricted model-authored actionPath to router.push, while AI provider failures return successful fallback bodies and Intel/Ask lack complete abort parity
defects.reproduction: Return an unexpected actionPath from the companion model or force a provider failure during Intel/Ask. The client trusts the path, and provider failure can appear as a successful assistant response without server Sentry capture; in-flight requests can outlive replacement/unmount.
defects.root_cause: No shared internal-route allowlist, no typed degraded response contract, and abort lifecycle exists only for part of the companion surface
defects.fix: Planned Wave 15.4: validate route actions on server and client against one internal registry, return typed degraded/error state with safe telemetry, and add AbortController parity across Focus, Intel, and Ask
defects.regression_test: pending Wave 15.4 route allowlist, degraded response, and abort lifecycle tests

defects.summary: Initial engine ADR replaced Brickrise's binding Phaser requirement with custom Canvas and required proof of Canvas failure before allowing Phaser
defects.reproduction: Compare the initial ADR 0004 decision and VECTOR engine table against the supplied Brickrise contract requiring a full-screen Phaser 2D platformer
defects.root_cause: Simplification pass treated a binding technology requirement as an optional architecture choice
defects.fix: Use one shared route-isolated Phaser runtime for Brickrise and other suitable 2D action/physics games, one Three.js runtime for 3D, and keep Second Sense engine-free
defects.regression_test: Plan search and adversarial rereview against source brief lines 413-429
defects.verification: ADR 0004 and VECTOR plan now explicitly require Phaser for Brickrise

defects.summary: Initial VECTOR plan compressed each game into one matrix row and omitted binding mechanics needed for later completion audit
defects.reproduction: Read docs/vector/PLAN.md before repair; details such as Time to Fly no-preview and stable retry positions, MiniTown exact shared-block roads, and Paper Glider procedural-room effects were absent
defects.root_cause: Summary matrix was incorrectly treated as the complete acceptance source while the external attachment is not a repository artifact
defects.fix: Add complete binding title-specific acceptance sections for all nine games while retaining the matrix as an index
defects.regression_test: Requirement-by-requirement comparison against source brief lines 367-580
defects.verification: All named game mechanics, originality constraints, inputs, persistence, performance, and sequencing requirements now live in docs/vector/PLAN.md

defects.summary: Program state and wave log described Wave 15.0 as landed before its commit existed
defects.reproduction: Inspect uncommitted PROGRAM_STATE and wave log while HEAD remains 845bfac9
defects.root_cause: Landing status was written prospectively instead of following successful commit evidence
defects.fix: Keep status in_progress/pending commit through review and first commit; update to landed only after commit succeeds, then amend the same wave commit
defects.regression_test: Pre-commit state assertion plus post-commit state assertion
defects.verification: Initial wave commit succeeded; landing status was then amended into that same commit so one wave remains one commit

defects.summary: Prompt manifests promised tool/model provenance but omitted the model field
defects.reproduction: Compare .prompts README contracts with initial manifest entries
defects.root_cause: Image-generation tool did not expose an underlying model identifier and the manifest omitted that fact
defects.fix: Record model as runtime-managed-not-exposed-by-tool rather than inventing an identifier
defects.regression_test: jq assertion that every prompt entry has non-empty tool and model fields
defects.verification: All three prompt records include explicit tool and honest model provenance
